title: payena dasyphylla
intro: Payena dasyphylla, a plant species native to Malaysia and Indonesia, is a type of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Payena dasyphylla include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's extracts are often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional remedies for skin and hair care.
